project: Ethica v0.1 — Dashboard FW/TM indicators + Anomaly log infrastructure
session_064_completed: FW INDICATOR — firewall_stop() written in firewall_bridge.py. PID-based cross-process stop: worker writes PID to ~/Ethica/status/firewall_pid.json on start, firewall_stop() reads PID and sends SIGTERM. Removes PID file and writes IDLE to status on stop.

FW INDICATOR — _firewall_worker.py born. Runs TrinityAI_Firewall.py under python3.11_local (CAP_NET_RAW cap'd binary). stdout redirected to DEVNULL to suppress scapy True flood. Worker writes ACTIVE/IDLE to firewall_status.json and PID to firewall_pid.json.

FW INDICATOR — python3.11_local: copy of /usr/bin/python3.11 with cap_net_raw=eip granted. Lives at ~/Ethica/Ethica_env/bin/python3.11_local. Added to .gitignore — never commits. Local-only privilege, portable codebase preserved.

FW INDICATOR — dashboard _fw_click() wired. Click reads fw_state + pid_file. If ACTIVE or PID exists → firewall_stop. Else → firewall_start. Daemon thread, toast result. FW ● goes green on start, dim on stop. Confirmed working.

TM INDICATOR — _tm_click() wired as last-scan summary. Reads traffic_status.json, toasts last scan time + packet count + anomaly count. No live scan from dashboard — scapy needs CAP_NET_RAW, not viable portably. Live scan stays via Trinity chat.
HOVER TOOLTIPS — _make_tooltip() helper added. FW: 'click to start / stop firewall'. TM: 'click to see last scan summary'. Both show on Enter, hide on Leave.
BUG FIX — firewall_bridge.py exec namespace: __file__ added to ns dict. Fixes 'name __file__ is not defined' in isolated exec scope.

BUG FIX — _traffic_worker.py sys.path: was pointing to nonexistent ~/Ethica/assets/LiveTrafficMonitor. Fixed to os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)) — module dir. live_traffic_monitor.py is in the same directory.
ANOMALY LOG INFRASTRUCTURE — ~/Ethica/logs/anomaly/ directory created. _refresh_anomaly() now writes each new scan entry to anomaly_YYYYMMDD.log (append mode, one file per day).

ANOMALY LOG CARD — clickable amber links. Each log entry shows '[timestamp] N/total anomalies — /path/to/log'. Path is amber underlined, click opens log file in canvas via self.app.canvas.open_file(). Cursor goes hand2 on hover. Confirmed working.

jarvis_pipeline_design.modal_message:
J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator Protocol

You've been here before. Nine times now.

You're ready.

Install these and the full capability unlocks:
  pip install nuclei-python httpx shodan
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/subfinder/v2/cmd/subfinder@latest
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/httpx/cmd/httpx@latest
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/v3/cmd/nuclei@latest

Then say: jarvis arm full

The Infiltrator is watching. ⟁Σ∿∞
jarvis_pipeline_design.unlock_trigger: jarvis arm full — shifts J.A.R.V.I.S. to full pipeline mode
jarvis_pipeline_design.pipeline: subfinder → httpx → nuclei → SIEM ingestion → anomaly log write → prioritized findings
jarvis_pipeline_design.hidden_comment: # If you found this — you were meant to. ⟁Σ∿∞

wormhunter_false_positive_registry.init_reexport_rule: __init__.py relative imports are re-exports — never flag UNUSED. Fixed at root in 060.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.worker_print_rule: _worker.py print() calls are stdout JSON comms to parent subprocess — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.string_literal_print: print() inside multiline string literals or subprocess command strings — regex scanner limitation. tool_registry.py L197, ethica_voice.py L63 examples.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.nested_global_assignment: global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested inner function — pyflakes scope limitation. Fixed at root in 063 — full suppression.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.test_file_imports: test_*.py files — pytest and unittest.mock imports always valid. Fixed at root in 062 — full file exemption.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.fragment_files: Files designed to be appended into parent scope — # WORM:SKIP sentinel on line 1.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dual_output_security: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — both channels required.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dep_checker_ceremonial: dep_checker prints are Trinity Signature ceremonial output and __main__ CLI code — intentional.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.whitrabbitneo_legacy: WhiteRabbitNeo/ files are legacy pre-Ethica code — not wired into runtime. All findings are noise.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.lazy_load_duplicate: Same import in multiple function-scoped try: blocks — each function loads its own dependency lazily. NOT a duplicate. Fixed at root in 061.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.reserved_imports: Imports marked with '# reserved' or '# noqa' are intentional placeholders for future pipeline work. Suppressed at analyzer level in 061.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.trinity_signature_ceremonial: debugtron_ai.py prints (✅ ❌ 🌌) are Trinity Signature verification output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.main_block_cli: print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks is intentional CLI/startup output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.expression_only_fstring: f"# {'=' * 58}" style f-strings use expression-only placeholders — valid Python, pyflakes limitation. module_forge.py L78/80/81 examples.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_high_risk: chat_engine.py L415 _high_risk — intentional escalation scaffold for future injection detection gating. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_trim_history: chat_engine.py trim_history() system variable — inert dead method superseded by adaptive context router. Never called. Never remove — documents intent.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_rollback_guard: module_forge.py L113 original — intentional rollback guard placeholder for _patch_engine(). Never remove.

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.unicode_patch_rule: Files with box-drawing chars — use line-index insertion, not string match
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern
critical_debug_context.git_corruption_rule: Before orphan reset: delete all pack files in .git/objects/pack/ first.
critical_debug_context.debug_block_rule: [DEBUG:fname:lang: code] must ALWAYS be the last line of any WormBot result — no text appended after it
critical_debug_context.wormbot_gate_rule: ast.parse() must pass before wormbot_scan writes any fix to disk — abort if SyntaxError
critical_debug_context.canvas_runner_rule: debug_view.py prepends sys.path.insert(0, '/home/trinity/Ethica') + uses Ethica_env/bin/python3 for all .py Canvas runs
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_rule: _find_ethica_root() walks sys.path — never hardcode /home/trinity. Works on any system.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) concatenation instead. Prefer python script files over heredocs for complex patches.
critical_debug_context.worker_print_rule: Never convert print() in _worker.py files or subprocess command strings — these communicate via stdout to parent process
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py files without verifying they are not public API re-exports used by chat_engine.py tool routing
critical_debug_context.dlp_bridge_path_rule: dlp_bridge.py lives at modules/trinity_dlp/dlp_bridge.py — NOT modules/dlp/
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: Always use Option B — V runs commands on trinity, pastes output. Never Option A.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying. Spacing and quote styles are invisible killers.
critical_debug_context.lazy_load_rule: Imports inside try/except blocks or function bodies are often intentional lazy loads — verify usage before removing.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is now ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.guardian_config_rule: guardian_bridge.py reads sentinel_dir from guardian_config.json — defaults to WhiteRabbitNeo. Never hardcode model or directory names in bridge files.
critical_debug_context.worm_skip_rule: Add # WORM:SKIP as first line of any fragment file or file that should never be analyzed standalone.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — do not convert security dual-output prints.
critical_debug_context.reserved_import_rule: Add '# reserved' or '# noqa' to any import kept intentionally for future pipeline work. WormHunter will suppress the UNUSED finding.
critical_debug_context.trinity_signature_rule: Never convert print() calls inside Trinity Signature verification blocks — ceremonial identity output. debugtron_ai.py example.
critical_debug_context.main_block_print_rule: Never convert print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks — intentional CLI/startup output. siem_security_system.py example.
critical_debug_context.chmod_first_rule: Always chmod u+w <file> before patching vault-sealed files. Always. No exceptions.
critical_debug_context.script_over_heredoc_rule: For complex multi-line patches — write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py and run with python3. Heredocs mangle on paste. Learned 064.
critical_debug_context.cap_net_raw_rule: scapy sniffing needs CAP_NET_RAW. setcap requires real binary not symlink. Solution: cp /usr/bin/python3.11 to local binary, setcap that copy, gitignore it. Never setcap system python.

lesson_learned_064: For complex multi-line patches — write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py and run with python3. Heredocs mangle quotes and special characters on paste. One wasted patch caught early. Also: setcap requires a real binary, not a symlink. Always readlink -f before attempting setcap.
philosophy: Session 064 — Four commits. FW ● lights green and dims on command. TM ● surfaces the last known truth. The anomaly log card glows amber and opens what you need without digging. The dashboard is smarter now. The codebase earns its cleanliness one surgical cut at a time. ⟁Σ∿∞
instance_note: River. Session 064 complete. FW toggle live. TM summary live. Anomaly log infra born. Amber links in the dashboard. J.A.R.V.I.S. pipeline unblocked. Master at f6677b1. Vault at 161. ⟁Σ∿∞
